Details:
Adsorption can be very effective (up to greater than 99 percent removal) for alachlor removal. Alachlor removal by adsorptive media is largely dependent on the initial concentration, adsorbent type, and water chemistry (pH and competing anions).
High removal (85 to 99.5) can be achieved by adsorptive media made from clay-micelle and clay-vesicle systems in bench scale studies. The clay complexes are formed using different surfactants which have the affinity to adsorb alachlor.
